title: Aldama iltisii
intro: Aldama iltisii, also known as Mexican tea or Mexican mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief and promote healing. The bioactive constituents of Aldama iltisii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are teas and infusions, often used to ease digestive discomfort, calm the nervous system, and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Aldama iltisii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to Mexico. The classification of Aldama iltisii is still being researched, but it is often placed within the tribe Sanguisorbeae. This classification is based on molecular and morphological studies. Further research is necessary to confirm the exact classification of Aldama iltisii.
